evidence_quotes.quote: licensing trend of videogames that defines them as a service, and not as a product.
the company can shut down at any time their services and the game itself can become abandonware.

paragraphs.text: I want to have the videogames digital protection reviewed. First, there has been an uprising concern about the licensing trend of videogames that defines them as a service, and not as a product. By doing so, the company can shut down at any time their services and the game itself can become abandonware. Should the company stop giving support to the videogame, it should be stated that the people that bought it should have the right to play via offline mode or through custom hosted servers that give no liability nor enforcement to the company that stopped support. This way the players can keep what they played for, and the company no longer needs to support it. But this can only happen if the videogames become a product again, and not a service that can be discontinued at any time.
